research.title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
research.summary: Chen Tang, Yizhou Wang, Jianyu Wu et al. — Structure-property relationships are foundational to biology, chemistry and materials science, where function, reactivity and physical response emerge from spatial, chemical and periodic organization.

research.title: Co-LMLM: Continuous-Query Limited Memory Language Models
research.summary: Yair Feldman, Linxi Zhao, Nathan Godey et al. — Limited memory language models (LMLMs) externalize factual knowledge during pretraining to a knowledge base (KB), rather than memorizing it in their weights. During generation, the model then fetches knowledge from the…

research.title: The Key to Going Linear: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization
research.summary: Anna Kuzina, Paul N. Whatmough, Babak Ehteshami Bejnordi — The quadratic cost of causal self-attention severely bottlenecks long-context transformer inference. While numerous post hoc linearization pipelines exist, it is difficult to identify which components preserve model…

research.title: From Noisy Traces to Root Causes: Structural Trajectory Analysis and Causal Extraction for Agent Optimization
research.summary: Ying Chang, Jiahang Xu, Xuan Feng et al. — The optimization of long-horizon agents increasingly relies on reflection-based mechanisms, where a large language model (LLM) acts as an optimizer to diagnose agent failures and improve agent policies.

research.title: Breaking Database Lock-in: Agentic Regeneration of High Performance Storage Readers for Database Bypass
research.summary: Victor Giannakouris, Immanuel Trummer — Analytical workloads operating on data stored in external database systems face a fundamental bottleneck: data access is guarded entirely by the database driver, like JDBC or ODBC, forcing all reads through query…

research.title: Institutional Red-Teaming: Deployment Rules, Not Just Models, Causally Shape Multi-Agent AI Safety
research.summary: Yujiao Chen — We introduce institutional red-teaming, an evaluation methodology for testing deployment rules in multi-agent AI: hold the agents, objectives, and task state fixed, vary only one rule, and attribute the resulting change…

research.title: Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage-Based Replay for Sample-Efficient Diffusion RLHF
research.summary: Eric Zhu, Abhinav Shrivastava, Soumik Mukhopadhyay — Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for aligning generative models with human preferences. However, applying RLHF to diffusion models remains highly feedback inefficient,…

research.title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning
research.summary: Vladislav Beliaev — Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (e.g. GRPO) is the engine behind today's reasoning models, yet it grades only the final answer.

research.summary: Shreyasvi Natraj, Cyrus Achtari, Felice Gragnano et al. — Electrocardiography (ECG) is one of the most widely used tests for diagnosing cardiovascular disease. Yet several remote clinics still utilize paper ECG printouts for their analysis due to limited connectivity and…

research.summary: Xiangming Huang, Guannan Zhang, Lu Lu et al. — The inverse design of physical systems governed by partial differential equations is computationally demanding due to the high dimensionality and non-convexity of design spaces. Generative models for inverse design…

research.title: Any-Dimensional Learning by Sampling
research.summary: Eitan Levin, Venkat Chandrasekaran — Many machine learning models are defined for inputs of different sizes, such as point clouds containing different numbers of points, sequences of tokens of different lengths, and graphs on different numbers of nodes.…

research.summary: Xinyi Wu, Siyuan Liu, Ali Jadbabaie — Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) provide transformers with a fixed grid of positional frequencies, yet trained models use these frequencies highly non-uniformly. We study what determines this frequency usage and…

labs.title: Our approach to government and national security partnerships
labs.summary: Learn how OpenAI approaches government and national security partnerships, with principles for responsible AI use, democratic accountability, and public safety.

labs.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
labs.summary: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

labs.title: Helping K–12 educators build practical AI skills
labs.summary: OpenAI Academy and the Walton Family Foundation are bringing hands-on AI Skills Jams to help K–12 educators build practical AI skills for the classroom.

labs.title: Introducing GPT-Live
labs.summary: A new generation of voice models for natural human-AI interaction, now powering ChatGPT Voice.

labs.title: The power of collaboration: How we can reduce traffic congestion
labs.summary: During “treatment” days, the modified routing guided all trips that encountered the pre-selected congested segments toward alternative routes with similar travel times. Estimated outcomes on trip travel times, speeds, and estimated emissions. Bottom: Histogram of net volume changes on individual segments.

labs.title: Australian Payments Plus moves faster with ChatGPT and Codex
labs.summary: See how Australian Payments Plus uses ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to move faster through payments complexity. AP+ saves time, improves quality, and keeps human judgment central.

labs.title: MUFG aims to become AI-native with OpenAI
labs.summary: MUFG uses ChatGPT Enterprise to build an AI-native organization, improve workflows, and deliver new AI-powered financial services at scale.

labs.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
labs.summary: Today, Google DeepMind and A24 are announcing a first-of-its-kind partnership focused on research. This partnership creates a deep research and development collaboration between A24 and Google DeepMind spanning multiple projects over time. This hands-on collaboration provides Google DeepMind with invaluable feedback and guidance from leading artists.

labs.title: Expanding our Heat Resilience data to 50+ global cities
labs.summary: This dataset is being made accessible through a new high-resolution Heat Resilience Earth Engine App. This dataset is open and accessible through our new, high-resolution Heat Resilience Earth Engine App . This platform provides high-resolution rooftop albedo (reflectivity) data to empower proactive municipal planning.

labs.title: Start building with Nano Banana 2 Lite and Gemini Omni Flash
labs.summary: We’re making it easier to experiment and scale your ideas with Nano Banana 2 Lite, our fastest, most cost-efficient Gemini Image model, and Gemini Omni Flash for high-quality video generation and conversational editing. Today, Gemini Omni Flash (gemini-omni-flash-preview) is rolling to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio, natively supporting high-quality video generation and conversational editing…

labs.title: Introducing TabFM: A zero-shot foundation model for tabular data
labs.summary: We introduce TabFM, a new foundation model for tabular data to simplify classification and regression workflows. Today, we introduce TabFM, a foundation model designed specifically for tabular data classification and regression. By framing tabular prediction as an ICL problem, TabFM eliminates the need for manual model training, hyperparameter tuning , and complex feature engineering.

labs.title: How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
labs.summary: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

startups.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules
startups.summary: On Wednesday, CEO Rishit Jhunjhunwala (pictured above) took to X to publicly challenge the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI), accusing the watchdog of preventing Truecaller from displaying community-reported spam information for calls from the country’s dedicated 1400 and 1600 number series, a restriction he said had enabled abuse of those numbers and eroded trust in legitimate business calls. TRAI later…

startups.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement
startups.summary: District Judge Sparkle Sooknanan noted that her court would accept the settlement, Bloomberg reported Wednesday, which cited her court opinion . Sooknanan previously questioned whether Musk was receiving “special treatment” from the Trump administration. Musk helped to bankroll Trump’s campaign during the 2024 presidential race.

startups.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
startups.summary: Lovable, a Swedish vibe-coding startup, is in talks to raise $300 million at a valuation of $13.2 billion — exactly double the $6.6 billion valuation the company achieved last December, Sifted reported . Other high-profile vibe-coding startups include Replit, valued at $9 billion in March, and Factory, a startup that helps enterprises develop AI agents, which raised $150 million at a $1.5 billion valuation in April.…

startups.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
startups.summary: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) Administrator Jonathan Morrison issued a directive Wednesday to autonomous vehicle (AV) developers, stating that it is unacceptable for their vehicles to interfere with first responders or law enforcement. Morrison noted in the letter that the agency has “identified a clear pattern of driverless AVs interfering with law enforcement and other first responders,”…

startups.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
startups.summary: Google’s SynthID system has been used to debunk a high-profile AI-generated hoax image, in a rare but significant win for the system. The image was shared widely on Reddit and X , but by Wednesday, the revered fact-checking site Snopes had debunked the image, noting that, when checked, the image registers as containing the SynthID watermark designed by Google to identify AI-generated pictures. Because the signature…

startups.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
startups.summary: One of the beneficiaries is QuantumDiamonds , a German startup that applies a novel approach to inspecting chips. CEO Kevin Berghoff told TechCrunch that raising the round was a fairly quick process, as QuantumDiamonds was able to demonstrate customer pull. “[QuantumDiamonds] can become Europe’s next ASML,” World Fund managing partner Daria Saharova wrote in a statement.

startups.title: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plots major US expansion
startups.summary: Manna Aero, the Ireland-based autonomous drone delivery startup, has been a smaller player in the United States. Manna has a hybrid business model. Manna isn’t entirely new to the United States.

Opus 4.7 , by comparison, costs $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. OpenAI has tiered costs for different model versions: Sol, its most expensive, costs $5 for 1 million input tokens and $30 for 1…

startups.title: ‘Slow-cial’ app Roost forces you to slow down to the speed of a carrier pigeon

startups.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
startups.summary: Before OpenAI’s GPT-3 ushered in the era of foundation models, companies built specialized natural language processing models from scratch, training each on large amounts of task-specific data. Pim de Witte, CEO of General Intuition , thinks embodied AI will follow a similar pattern. Rather than collecting huge real-world datasets to build specialized robot models, he argues the industry should focus on better…

startups.title: Elon Musk says X will send DMs when posts you’ve engaged with are corrected
startups.summary: X’s crowdsourced fact-checking system, Community Notes, will be updated to send users direct messages alerting them whenever a post they have interacted with has received a correction. Instead, Community Notes contributors could suggest corrections and add critical details or missing information to posts. This weakens Community Notes as a system that surfaces information when it’s most needed, critics have pointed…

startups.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
startups.summary: Google Photos is getting a new “Video Remix” feature that can edit and transform videos in seconds, Google announced on Wednesday. “Creating beautiful video clips shouldn’t require professional skills or hours of editing,” Google wrote in the blog post. The feature is the latest in a series of AI-powered updates introduced to Google Photos.

wider_tech.title: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
wider_tech.summary: The ‘super sensing’ glasses could continuously record audio and frequently snap photos. The ‘super sensing’ glasses could continuously record audio and frequently snap photos. Should these glasses or the “super sensing” features be released, they would raise significant privacy concerns.

wider_tech.title: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
wider_tech.summary: Samsung is announcing new foldables soon, and you can save $30 if you register your interest ahead of time. Samsung is announcing new foldables soon, and you can save $30 if you register your interest ahead of time. If you register your interest ahead of time and end up preordering one of the new phones shortly after they’re announced, Samsung will give you a $30 store credit at checkout.

wider_tech.title: Microsoft’s Xbox reset is pivoting Obsidian to make Fallout instead of Avowed

wider_tech.title: America’s cheapest new EV is smaller than a ping-pong table and tops out at 19mph
wider_tech.summary: The Fiat Topolino may only cost $13,995, but you’re not really buying a car for that money. The Fiat Topolino may only cost $13,995, but you’re not really buying a car for that money. The Fiat Topolino is basically the ultimate embodiment of those tradeoffs.

wider_tech.title: Cockroaches will learn to fear my SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable
wider_tech.summary: The SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable’s capabilities are identical to the original SwitchBot Bot that costs a little less. The Bot also includes a small plastic attachment that sticks directly to your light switch. The SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable is a clever, relatively inexpensive solution.

wider_tech.title: If Microsoft sold off Xbox, who would even buy it?
wider_tech.summary: This week, Microsoft took a huge ax to its Xbox business. We don’t know what Microsoft’s asking price for Xbox would be. Microsoft acquired Activision Blizzard in a $68.7 billion deal .

wider_tech.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro is a great travel companion, and it’s on sale for $40
wider_tech.summary: The Pro lets you use wireless earbuds with in-flight entertainment systems, and it’s at its best price in over a year. The Pro lets you use wireless earbuds with in-flight entertainment systems, and it’s at its best price in over a year. The AirFly Pro is useful in other ways, too.

wider_tech.title: ChatGPT’s upgraded voice mode is better at shutting up
wider_tech.summary: The new GPT-Live-1 model can speak and listen at the same time — and interrupt you less. The new GPT-Live-1 model can speak and listen at the same time — and interrupt you less. During a press briefing, OpenAI research lead Kundan Kumar called GPT-Live-1 the company’s “smartest voice model” yet.

wider_tech.title: The whole Pixel line could get more expensive this year
wider_tech.summary: The Pixel 11 could drop the 128GB storage configuration, while the Pixel Watch 5 may cost $50 more than last year’s model. The Pixel 11 could drop the 128GB storage configuration, while the Pixel Watch 5 may cost $50 more than last year’s model. Last year’s base Pixel 10 model cost €899 with 128GB of storage, while the Pixel 10 Pro started at €1,099.
